Ophrys celiensis is a rare and endemic species found in the Iberian Peninsula. It is a type of terrestrial orchid that blooms in late spring with distinctive flowers that resemble bees. As a member of the Orchidaceae family, Ophrys celiensis shares characteristics with other orchid species, such as showy flowers and adapted pollination mechanisms.
